decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.16°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at a moderate level relative to its upper bound. Increasing conductivity by 40% (within the allowed 2.0x ratio) will enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the local hot spot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature violation.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Conductivity value 700.0 exceeds the documented upper bound of 500.0 in the variable description; if strictly enforced, cap at 500.0 or adjust geometry instead.
Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
